narrative:
Based on the available article excerpts, several health policy developments are emerging in early March 2026. The Trump administration appears to be navigating ongoing vaccine policy controversies while attempting to pivot toward more popular health initiatives [1]. According to STAT News reporting, the administration is "trying to shift focus away from controversial vaccine policies toward topics that are popular with voters, such as lowering drug prices and encouraging healthy diets," though "vaccines keep coming up in the health care news cycle" [1].

Concurrently, there are developments in drug pricing policy, with the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services (CMS) reportedly expanding a Medicaid "most-favored nations" pilot program to include more pharmaceutical manufacturers [2]. This appears connected to broader Trump administration drug pricing initiatives, with one report noting that the "most-favored nation" policy "is starting to look less like a slogan and more like a real pressure point that could mean higher prices abroad" [3].

The vaccine policy landscape includes criticism from prominent figures in the medical community. Vaccine researcher Stanley Plotkin, described as a "93-year-old vaccine legend," has reportedly characterized the "anti-vaccine turn inside government" as both "stupid" and "immoral" while observing declining vaccination rates [3].

**Note**: The provided articles are primarily newsletter previews and subscription prompts, limiting the depth of factual detail available for analysis.

citations.title: STAT+: Trump can't break free from vaccine politics
citations.quote: The Trump administration is trying to shift focus away from controversial vaccine policies toward topics that are popular with voters, such as lowering drug prices and encouraging healthy diets. But vaccines keep coming up in the health care news cycle.

citations.title: STAT+: A Merck cancer drug to watch
citations.quote: 93-year-old vaccine legend Stanley Plotkin is watching vaccination rates slip and calling the anti-vaccine turn inside government what he thinks it is: 'stupid' and 'immoral.' Also... the policy is starting to look less like a slogan and more like a real pressure point that could mean higher prices abroad.

keyDevelopments: Trump administration attempting to shift focus from vaccine controversies to drug pricing and nutrition policies
CMS expanding Medicaid 'most-favored nations' pilot program to include more drugmakers
Vaccine expert Stanley Plotkin criticizing government anti-vaccine policies amid declining vaccination rates
Trump administration's 'most-favored nation' drug pricing policy showing real implementation effects
